spec_match.rationale: The page successfully lets visitors programmatically probe named-feature() and other conditional rules from JavaScript using CSS.supports().

interactive.rationale: Features tabs, custom input, presets, and a batch results table that updates dynamically with live CSS.supports() evaluations.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Matches the index blurb by demonstrating JavaScript CSSOM querying equivalents of named-feature @supports conditional rules.

edge_cases.rationale: The property presets (display: grid and container-type: inline-size) do not include surrounding parentheses, causing them to evaluate to false in standard browsers when evaluated in the single-argument CSS.supports() call.

references.rationale: The page is missing the required chromestatus.com/feature/5153932394102784 reference link in its 'see also' section.

design_system.rationale: Follows showcase design styles, featuring elegant tabs, interactive inputs, and proper contrast ratios for supported/unsupported badges.

openQuestions.title: Wrap property-value presets in parentheses
openQuestions.detail: The quick presets 'prop-grid' (display: grid) and 'prop-container' (container-type: inline-size) are loaded into the input as-is and evaluated using single-argument CSS.supports(). Since the one-argument form expects a condition string, property declarations must be enclosed in parentheses. Modify the preset strings to be parenthesized (e.g. '(display: grid)' and '(container-type: inline-size)') so they evaluate to true.

openQuestions.title: Cite the ChromeStatus entry link on the concept page
openQuestions.detail: The 'see also' section lists internal links but does not cite the official chromestatus.com/feature/5153932394102784 reference link. Add the required external link to fulfill showcase conformance guidelines.
